summary:
The articular disc of the TMJ is thickest at its rear end and tapers inward; this structure supports jaw movements through interactions such as those involving the lateral pterygoid. 

Key Points Addressed:
- Thickness distribution
- Central tapering  
- Dynamic support mechanism via specific muscles

This summary captures the essential anatomical features without extraneous details or assumptions beyond what was provided. \boxed{}
